privileged_info_summary: The artwork 'The Great Wave off Kanagawa' by Katsushika Hokusai (1760-1849) is a famous Japanese woodblock print created in late 1831, part of the 'Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji' series. The composition features three boats in the foreground, a dominant cresting wave in the center, and Mount Fuji in the background. The work demonstrates all claimed elements of art: dynamic curving lines (especially at wave tips), innovative Prussian blue coloring layered with indigo, geometric shapes (spiral wave, triangular mountain), textured surface from pigment layering and printing technique, sharp visual contrast between elements, and powerful sense of movement through compositional flow. The metadata claims about required compositional terms (foreground, background, wave) and art element terms (line, color, shape, texture, contrast, movement) are all verified as accurate descriptions of this artwork. Both image source URLs (Wikimedia Commons and The Metropolitan Museum) are functional and provide valid high-resolution images of the artwork.
